surface.name: Teleport Community Edition local Docker welcome screen
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the official Community Edition local Docker route is primary because it is public, open source, no-account, and avoids the gated Enterprise Cloud signup. The guide provides exact Docker, TLS, install, configure, start, and welcome-screen checkpoint steps.

alternatives_considered.name: Community Edition Linux demo
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The overview recommends this for self-hosting locally or free use, while Enterprise Cloud is selected for quick evaluation.

alternatives_considered.name: Enterprise Self-Hosted
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The overview says this paid compliance-focused route requires a valid license and contact with Teleport.

entry_point.developer_intent: Launch a Teleport cluster for a quick evaluation.
assumed_prior_state: No Teleport account, tenant, cluster, local user, or recovery codes exist.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A local Docker container runs Teleport Community Edition and serves the Teleport Web UI over HTTPS.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Opening `https://localhost:3080` displays the Teleport welcome screen.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Confirm that you can access the Teleport Web UI and see the welcome screen.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The welcome screen is the guide's first explicit checkpoint and proves the Auth, Proxy, and SSH services started and the HTTPS endpoint is reachable.

prerequisites.requirement: Docker is installed and can run containers.
mkcert is installed so a local certificate authority and localhost certificate can be created.
Local port 3080 is available to map to the container HTTPS listener.

primary_path.action: Open the Community Edition deployment guide and select Local Docker container.
primary_path.output: The local route and checkpoint are visible.

primary_path.action: Install mkcert, create `teleport-tls`, install the local CA, generate a localhost certificate, and copy `rootCA.pem` into the directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct mkcert installation or certificate paths.

primary_path.action: Start the documented Ubuntu container with the TLS directory mounted and port 3080 mapped to 443.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Resolve Docker mount or port conflicts.

primary_path.action: Install curl, trust the copied mkcert CA, and run the official Teleport install script inside the container.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check network access and CA file paths.

primary_path.action: Run `teleport configure` with cluster name localhost, public address localhost:443, and the mounted certificate and key.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct certificate filenames and mount location.

primary_path.output: Teleport Auth, Proxy, and SSH services start.
primary_path.success_signal: The process remains running and listens through mapped port 3080.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Review terminal logs for certificate or port errors.

primary_path.action: Open `https://localhost:3080`, accept the Community Edition terms, and verify the welcome screen.
primary_path.output: The Teleport Web UI welcome screen is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide's Verify Teleport is running checkpoint passes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check container status, port 3080, and Teleport logs.

branches.condition: The developer prefers a free local self-hosted test.
branches.path: Use the Community Edition Linux demo route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the trial and managed provisioning with Linux or Docker installation and a local welcome-screen checkpoint.

branches.condition: The organization requires self-hosting for compliance.
branches.path: Contact Teleport for Enterprise Self-Hosted and obtain a valid license.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a sales and licensing path before deployment.

friction_gates.description: Community Edition local Docker is selected over Enterprise Cloud and Enterprise Self-Hosted under workshop fastest/most-used local-first policy.

sources.title: Step 1 - Sign up for Teleport Enterprise Cloud | Teleport
Signup for Teleport Enterprise: Unify Access to All Your Environments | Teleport
